Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace config_env_var with config_env map (#16)

* Replace config_env_var with config_env map for value-aware env gating

The old `config_env_var` field only checked for presence of an env var
by name. The new `config_env` map requires specific key=value pairs to
match, giving precise control over which identity/config is active.

- config_env is a HashMap<String, String> — all entries must match (AND)
- Checks inline command env vars first, falls back to process env
- Uses shellexpand for correct ~/\$HOME/\$VAR expansion in config values
- Adds Claude Code review and @claude GitHub Actions workflows
- 367 tests (10 new for env_satisfies), zero clippy warnings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump version to 0.5.0

Breaking config change: config_env_var → config_env map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Guard env-mutating tests with require_nextest, log shellexpand failures

- Add require_nextest() guard that panics with a clear message when
  env-mutating tests are run under `cargo test` (shared process) instead
  of nextest (process-per-test isolation)
- Add collision proof tests (alpha/beta) that demonstrate the race:
  under cargo test they fail 100% of the time, under nextest they pass
- Log warn! when shellexpand::full fails on a config_env value, so
  users can diagnose typos like $HMOE via --log

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

fix: wrap useSearchParams in Suspense boundary for auth callback

Next.js 14 requires useSearchParams() to be inside a <Suspense>
boundary during static generation. Without it, the production build
fails with "useSearchParams() should be wrapped in a suspense boundary."

Extract the hook usage into a CallbackHandler child component and wrap
it in Suspense with a loading fallback.

Replace compile-time VITE_* env vars with runtime config served by the
Go static server from Cloud Run env vars. One image now promotes through
environments without rebuilding.

- deploy.yml: pass env vars to Cloud Run frontend via --set-env-vars,
  derive relay WebSocket URL from deploy output, add config health check

Requires adding GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID_DEV GitHub secret.

* fix: use json.Marshal for config values in buildConfigJS

Reviewer flagged that %q (Go string quoting) is not guaranteed to produce
valid JS for all inputs. Switch to encoding/json.Marshal which produces
spec-compliant JSON strings, strictly safe for JS embedding.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add PFC context gates: task-dependent gating of patch outputs

GRU state → per-patch sigmoid gate over hidden_dim. Like PFC selective
disinhibition — same patches learn shared computation, context gate
selects which dimensions are active for the current task. Enables
multi-task without interference. +66K params (64→256 per patch layer).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

research: add BU consequence chain - non-injectivity, no-retraction, Brouwer FP (borsuk-ulam-oq-03) (#3366)

- Axiomized: no_retraction, brouwer_fixed_point, lusternik_schnirelmann, invariance_of_dimension
- Documents the complete logical chain: BU → no odd map → no retraction → Brouwer FP
- 27 proved theorems, 0 sorries, 5 axioms total

repository.name: lean-genius
repository.description: Annotations for Lean Proofs

commit.message:
research: prove circuit necessity, strengthen Walk (konigsberg-oq-02) (#3368)

* research: formalize Ehrhart polynomials for 3D lattice polytopes (picks-theorem-oq-03)

- Complete Lean 4 formalization of Ehrhart's theorem (520+ lines)
- Axiomatized LatticePolytope structure with counting function
- Proved constant term = 1, coefficient interpretations
- Ehrhart-Macdonald reciprocity for interior point counting
- Unit cube verification: (n+1)³ = n³ + 3n² + 3n + 1
- Connection to Pick's theorem as 2D special case
- 3D coefficient structure: Vn³ + (S/2)n² + c₁n + 1
- Reeve tetrahedra dimension argument for Pick's failure in 3D
- Answers OQ-03 from Pick's theorem gallery entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: formalize directed Euler path characterization (konigsberg-oq-02)

- Directed graph infrastructure: Digraph, in-degree, out-degree, balanced
- Directed Eulerian circuit criterion: all vertices balanced (axiomatized)
- Directed Eulerian path criterion: source/sink degree conditions (axiomatized)
- Degree sum lemma: Σ indeg = Σ outdeg = |E|
- Concrete examples: directed C₃, C₄ (balanced), unbalanced (no circuit)
- Non-existence proof for unbalanced digraphs
- Path digraph A→B→C satisfies Euler path conditions
- Connection to undirected case and de Bruijn sequences

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: prove vector-valued mean value inequality (mean-value-theorem-oq-03)

Formalize the vector-valued MVT generalization: ‖f(x) - f(a)‖ ≤ C·(x-a) when
‖f'(t)‖ ≤ C on [a,b]. This replaces the scalar MVT equality with an inequality
that works in any normed space.

351 lines, 9 theorems, 2 axioms, 0 sorries. Key results:
- Mean value inequality from Mathlib's norm_image_sub_le_of_norm_deriv_le_segment'
- Scalar MVT as special case, classical equality strictly stronger
- Circular motion counterexample showing equality fails for vectors
- Zero derivative implies constant (vector-valued, proved)
- Contraction bounds, displacement bounds, ODE difference bounds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: extend CRT to ideal bridge and summary theorem (chinese-remainder-non-coprime-oq-02)

- Add Part IV: Ideal Bridge connecting element coprimality to ideal coprimality
  (IsCoprime a b ↔ Ideal.span {a} ⊔ Ideal.span {b} = ⊤)
- Add Euclid's lemma and coprime_mul_dvd_iff as consequences
- Add Part V: Summary theorem combining existence, uniqueness, and ideal characterization
- Add comprehensive docstrings throughout
- All theorems fully proved (0 sorries), verified via Docker build

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: eliminate sorry in ballot problem OQ-03, axiomatize Lindström involution (ballot-problem-oq-03)

Replace 4 sorry occurrences with 1 well-documented axiom (lindstrom_involution).
The axiom states the classical Lindström involution bijection: intersecting
identity path pairs biject with crossing path pairs via suffix-swapping at
the first shared lattice point.

Changes:
- Convert lindstrom_involution_card' sorry → axiom lindstrom_involution
- Fix lgv_lemma_2x2 proof to use complement counting + axiom (zify/ring)
- Remove duplicate Part XII lgv_lemma_2x2 (was shadowing Part VI version)
- Fix lindstrom_involution_card to delegate to axiom instead of sorry
- Update status: 0 sorries, 1 axiom (down from 4 sorries)

The involution infrastructure (splitAfterEast, swapTails, involutivity) is
fully proved. The remaining gap is constructing firstIntersectionColumn and
connecting it to produce the explicit Equiv.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: prove circuit necessity, strengthen Walk definition (konigsberg-oq-02)

- Convert directed_euler_circuit_necessary from axiom to theorem
  via source/target permutation argument (chain + circuit → Perm)
- Strengthen Walk structure with Chain' consecutive + ends_at fields
- Prove symmetric_digraph_balanced (new theorem)
- Replace trivial de Bruijn placeholders with substantive arc count formula
- 2 routine counting sorries remain (eulerian_source/target_count)
- Axiom count reduced from 6 to 4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix bot road strategy: stop fake blocking, race to good vertices instead

Bots were building roads to "block" opponents, but in Catan roads don't
block settlement placement — only the distance rule does. Now bots:
- Race toward high-value vertices when opponents are nearby (not block)
- Check distance rule in road path planning (skip unbuildable targets)
- Scale road-building priority by how good the target vertex actually is
  instead of blindly building roads when no settlement is reachable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Removed the useEffect that redirected users to /pricing when their
team had no planId, which was blocking dashboard access entirely.
Users can now navigate the dashboard freely regardless of plan status.

commit.message:
feat: make unmapped dataset types transparent in restructure_assets

When type_to_dir_map is provided and a dataset's type is not in the map
(e.g. "Split"), that dataset is now treated as a structural container and
does not contribute a directory path component. Its children still traverse
and use their own types for directory routing.

This allows passing a Split parent dataset (type "Split") directly to
restructure_assets instead of having to enumerate its Training/Testing
children — the children's types ("Training"→"train", "Testing"→"test")
produce clean paths without a "split/" prefix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pending crossings + diagnostics — resolve min_track_frames dropping LineZone counts

Root causes of new_crossings=0:
1. NameError in logger.debug (len(excl) → len(excl_polys)) caused _refresh WARNING but
   zone was still set so this was cosmetic only
2. min_track_frames=4 permanently drops crossings: sv.LineZone.trigger() fires True only
   once (the crossing frame); if track_frames < 4 at that moment, the crossing is lost
   forever. With process_every_n=2 a vehicle needs 8 raw frames before crossing counts.

Fixes:
- Pending crossings dict: when crossed_in/out fires but track_frames < min_tf,
  store {tid: direction_in} in _pending_crossings; flush in next frame when mature
- Cleanup: purge pending_crossings for stale tracks
- Diagnostic INFO log every 50 calls: line coords, detection centers (relative),
  track_frames, eligibility, crossed_in/out flags — visible in Railway logs
- LineZone.trigger() exception now logged at WARNING level

commit.message:
Fix LTK widget: inject script inside container element

The LTK ltkwidget.js script looks for its own <script> tag inside the
widget container div to derive the base URL. Using next/script placed it
outside the container, causing "Cannot read properties of null" at line 343.
Now manually inject the script element inside the container via useEffect.

commit.message:
fix: use git reset --hard in deploy to avoid local changes conflict

git pull fails if any tracked file (e.g. package.json) has local changes.
git fetch + git reset --hard always overwrites local state with remote,
preventing race conditions when manual npm installs modify tracked files.

commit.message:
Fix auto-updater: catch promise rejections on manual & periodic checks

The menu "Check for Updates..." click handler and periodic interval
were calling autoUpdater.checkForUpdates() without .catch(), causing
unhandled promise rejections that silently killed the update flow.
Extracted a shared doCheckForUpdates() that properly catches errors
and surfaces them via the error state.
